description: Image stacking is a fundamental technique for improving the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) in observational astronomy. Your task is to median-combine 25 calibrated CCD frames of a stellar field located at ~/AstroImages/stacking_project/frames/ and measure the SNR improvement on a target star identified in ~/AstroImages/stacking_project/target_info.txt. Save the resulting median-stacked image to ~/AstroImages/stacking_project/output/median_stack.fits. Calculate the improvement factor by comparing the SNR of the target star in the stacked image versus a single frame. Create a report file at ~/AstroImages/stacking_project/output/snr_report.txt containing the labeled values for SNR_single, SNR_stacked, Improvement_factor, and N_frames (25).
